mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services, making it ideal for applications requiring reliable and scalable storage solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to simpler storage solutions.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Rook using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Rook operator in the 'rook-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace rook-system

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

```

> Creates a namespace for Rook.
> Installs the Rook Ceph operator.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

steps.title: Create Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster by applying the custom resource definition (CRD) for Rook.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/cluster.yaml

```

> Deploys the example Ceph cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify Rook and Ceph Installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Rook operator and the Ceph cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system get pods

kubectl -n rook-ceph get cephcluster

```

> Lists all pods in the rook-system namespace to verify the operator is running.
> Verifies the status of the Ceph cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Ceph Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook Ceph operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Ceph cluster and any data stored within it.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-system

kubectl delete namespace rook-system

```

> Uninstalls the Rook operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Ceph Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step upgrades the Rook operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

kubectl -n rook-system uncordon <node-name>

```

> Drains the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades the Rook operator.
> Uncordons the node after upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to create ceph cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Ceph cluster configuration is incorrect or if there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster configuration and ensure that the nodes meet the resource requirements.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are issues with the Ceph configuration or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rook-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph cluster status is 'HEALTH_WARN'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are warnings in the cluster, possibly due to misconfigured pools or insufficient replicas.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it <ceph-mon-pod> -- ceph health detail' to get more information.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no available monitors'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ceph monitors are not running or reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the monitor pods with 'kubectl -n rook-ceph get pods' and ensure they are running.

versionNotes.changes: Improved stability and performance of the Ceph cluster, added support for new storage classes.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl -n rook-system get pods' showing all pods in a 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
